content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs (full file), the supporting modifications in src/test_support/br.rs, bv.rs, and fixtures.rs, plus the integration points in src/adapters/br_process.rs, br_models.rs, bv_process.rs, and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs. The fixture builder is correct: it materializes a Ready milestone with plan.md/plan.json, writes a 6-bead graph (root epic + 2 workstream epics + 3 tasks) to .beads/issues.jsonl with proper parent_child + blocks dependencies, and exposes mock br/bv adapters whose dispatch closures share an Arc<Mutex<ScenarioState>>. Reads (current_issues, next_ready_issue) reload from disk so external rewrites stay visible, and mutations (create_issue, add_dependency, close_issue) persist back via the shared write_bead_graph_issues helper from fixtures.rs (so a single source of truth for the on-disk shape is preserved). Working-directory validation rejects None or mismatched cwd. Command parsers (parse_create_issue_spec, parse_dep_add_args, parse_close_args) match the exact arg shapes produced by BrCommand::create/dep_add_with_kind/close, including --type=blocks vs --type=parent-child handling, and the test verifies real BrMutationAdapter and BvAdapter exec_json round-trips against the mocks (including external rewrite scenarios). The smoke test checks fixture completeness, plan.md/plan.json existence, Ready status, journal events (PlanDrafted, no PlanUpdated), bead-graph row count and dependency types, and end-to-end mutation flow including ready-bead progression. No critical, high, or medium bugs found. Minor non-blocking observations (not raised as amendments since prior cycles already iterated extensively): the elapsed-time assertion uses Duration::from_secs(10) rather than the spec's <1s target so a regression to several seconds would not be caught; validate_fixture_working_dir uses direct path equality which could surface as a false negative on macOS where /tmp is symlinked to /private/tmp; next_follow_up_index is internal state and would collide if an external writer creates a bead with the same id between create_issue calls. None of these are real bugs in the implemented scope.

## Amendments

None.
